Description:
The ZX-80 is a Robotek Defense Division platform built for military field operations. Originally deployed for reconnaissance, breach-and-clear, and asset extraction in contested zones, the line entered service in 2402 and was retired from active duty fifteen years later. Surviving units are rare; most were decommissioned or recovered by the manufacturer.

The chassis is modular by design. A baseline ZX-80 runs on standard bipedal locomotion and carries a single sidearm. Fully upgraded variants integrate jump amplifiers, magnetic wall climbers, and reaction thrusters, allowing traversal of vertical and unstable terrain that would defeat most service robots. The frame supports a wide range of weapon mounts and an embedded intrusion suite — capable of bypassing standard Robotek consumer-grade security and a respectable percentage of industrial systems.

Unlike domestic models, the ZX-80 was never marketed and never made public. Its existence has been confirmed in three federal hearings and denied in twelve press statements.

A retired ZX-80 in the field is, by definition, off-books. No party recovers them, no party reports them missing, and no party admits authorizing their original deployment.
